ClickHouse 中后台分布式调度任务池（BackgroundDistri: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse BackgroundDistributedSchedulePool. This pool is dedicated to handling asynchronously executed distributed data sending tasks
同步插入到 Distributed 表时超时: Timeouts on Synchronous Inserts into Distributed Tables
ClickHouse 中后台处理池（针对消息流处理的部分）当前激活的任务数量，专: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse background processing pool for message stream processing, focused on message broker related background tasks
ClickHouse 中后台缓冲区刷新调度任务池（BackgroundBuffe: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse BackgroundBufferFlushSchedulePool. This pool focuses on background tasks related to the periodic flushing of buffer data
ClickHouse 中后台获取任务池（BackgroundFetchesPoo: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse BackgroundFetchesPool.
ClickHouse 中后台调度任务池（BackgroundSchedulePo: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse BackgroundSchedulePool
ClickHouse中后台移动任务池（BackgroundMovePool）当前: Number of currently active tasks in the ClickHouse BackgroundMovePool
ClickHouse尝试与分布式表中的远程节点建立连接时失败并进行重试的总次数。: Total number of retried connection attempts that failed when ClickHouse tried to connect to a remote node of a Distributed table. It means that while running distributed queries or insert operations ClickHouse hit network problems, unreachable remote nodes or other connection errors, so the initial connection attempt failed and the system retried according to its configuration
MergeTree 表写入的压缩字节数: Compressed Bytes Written to MergeTree Tables
MergeTree 表插入的未压缩字节数: Uncompressed Bytes Inserted into MergeTree Tables
ReplicatedMergeTree表的数据分区成功进行合并的次数: Number of successful merges of data parts of ReplicatedMergeTree tables
ReplicatedMergeTree表的数据部分成功合并的次数: Number of successful fetches of data parts of ReplicatedMergeTree tables
与 TCP 服务器（带本地接口的客户端）的连接数，也包括服务器-服务器连接: Number of connections to the TCP server (clients with the native interface), also includes server-server connections
与失败的查询相同，但仅限于INSERT查询: Same as failed queries, but only for INSERT queries
与失败的查询相同，但仅限于SELECT查询: Same as failed queries, but only for SELECT queries
与查询数相同，但仅限于INSERT查询: Same as the number of queries, but only for INSERT queries
为当前运行的后台合并保留的磁盘空间。它略大于当前合并部分的总大小: Disk space reserved for currently running background merges. It is slightly larger than the total size of the parts being merged
从MergeTree表读取的所有数据部分中（非相邻）的范围数: Number of (non-adjacent) ranges in all data parts read from MergeTree tables
从MergeTree表读取的数据部分数: Number of data parts read from MergeTree tables
从MergeTree表读取的标记数（索引粒度）: Number of marks (index granules) read from MergeTree tables
从ReplicatedMergeTree表的复制数据合并失败次数: Number of failed fetches of replicated parts of ReplicatedMergeTree tables
从压缩源（文件，网络）读取的压缩块数（独立压缩的数据块）: Number of compressed blocks (independently compressed blocks of data) read from compressed sources (files, network)
从压缩源（文件，网络）读取的未压缩字节数（解压后的字节数）: Number of uncompressed bytes (bytes after decompression) read from compressed sources (files, network)
从所有表SELECT的字节数（未压缩列以它们在内存中存储的形式）: Bytes SELECTed from all tables (uncompressed columns in the form they are stored in memory)
从文件中进行慢查询读取的次数，这表明系统过载\n\ntrends\n计算过去2分:
Number of slow reads from files, which indicates that the system is overloaded

trends
Average per-second growth of slow read events over the last 2 minutes across all ClickHouse instances matching $instance
从文件描述符读取的字节数。如果文件是压缩的，这将显示压缩后的数据大小: Bytes read from file descriptors. If the file is compressed, this shows the compressed size of the data
从文件描述符读取（read/pread）失败的次数: Number of failed reads (read/pread) from file descriptors
从文件描述符进行读取（read/pread）的次数，不包括套接字: Number of reads (read/pread) from file descriptors, excluding sockets
从文件缓存中打开文件时没有命中缓存的次数: Number of cache misses when opening files from the file cache
包含了大量列或者说是字段广泛的数据。一个宽数据分区意味着单个数据块或行包含很多列: Contains a large number of columns, that is, a wide set of fields. A wide part means that a single data block or row contains many columns, which may affect the efficiency of data processing and queries, especially for selections or aggregations over many columns.
后台任务池中的活跃任务数: Active Tasks in the Background Pool
后台合并读取的未压缩字节数（列以它们在内存中存储的形式）。这是合并前的字节数: Uncompressed bytes read by background merges (columns in the form they are stored in memory). This is the number of bytes before merging
后台合并读取的行数。这是合并前的行数: Rows read by background merges. This is the number of rows before merging
后台消息代理调度任务池任务数: Background Message Broker Schedule Pool Tasks
后台缓冲区刷新调度任务池任务数: Background Buffer Flush Schedule Pool Tasks
向 Distributed 表插入数据块的操作因为待处理字节数量过高而被限制或延: Number of times block inserts into Distributed tables were throttled or delayed because too many bytes were pending. In ClickHouse, to avoid network congestion or excessive load on remote nodes caused by a sudden flood of data, the system applies flow control. When the pending data volume (in bytes) exceeds a threshold, new insert requests are not executed immediately but are held or queued until resources are released or the load decreases
向 Distributed 表插入数据块的操作因待处理字节数量过多而被拒绝的次数: Number of times block inserts into Distributed tables were rejected because too many bytes were pending, more precisely because the 'Too many bytes' exception was raised
向FD写入的次数，不包括套接字: Number of writes to file descriptors, excluding sockets
向MergeTree系列表（包括ReplicatedMergeTree等变种）中: Number of data blocks used when inserting data into MergeTree family tables (including variants such as ReplicatedMergeTree)
向远程服务器发送数据的连接数量: Number of connections that send data to remote servers
因超时而未能成功完成的同步插入Distributed表的操作: Synchronous inserts into Distributed tables that did not complete successfully because of a timeout
在ClickHouse进行所有重试尝试之后仍然未能成功建立到分布式表中远程节点的: Cumulative number of times ClickHouse still failed to establish a connection to a remote node of a Distributed table after all retry attempts. It means that when communicating with a node of the distributed cluster the system retried as configured but still could not establish the connection.
在ClickHouse集群中，由于特定情况而暂时处于只读状态的复制表数量。这种情:
Number of replicated tables in the ClickHouse cluster that are temporarily read-only for a specific reason. This usually happens in the following two situations:

Reinitialization after a lost ZooKeeper session: ClickHouse uses ZooKeeper to keep the cluster coordinated and consistent. If the ZooKeeper session is lost, the affected replicated tables may be switched to read-only automatically to prevent data inconsistency, until the connection to ZooKeeper is restored and the data is synchronized.

Started without ZooKeeper configured: if a ClickHouse instance starts without ZooKeeper properly configured, it may be unable to determine its exact state in the replication cluster and therefore, to be safe, sets the replicated tables to read-only to avoid potential data conflicts or inconsistency.
在因待处理字节数量过多而导致向 Distributed 表插入数据块的操作被限制: Average of the total number of milliseconds spent by all delayed insert operations while block inserts into Distributed tables were throttled because too many bytes were pending. This metric shows the actual time cost of insert delays and helps to understand the waiting time accumulated by flow control during data writes.
在处理ReplicatedMergeTree表时，我们选择从副本中直接下载已经合:
Number of times we chose to download an already merged data part from a replica instead of doing the merge ourselves when working with ReplicatedMergeTree tables. Normally ClickHouse prefers to run the merge locally in order to save network traffic. However, downloading an already merged part from a replica happens in two special cases:

Missing source parts required for the merge: when some of the source data parts required for a merge are missing locally, ClickHouse may fetch the already merged part from another replica to avoid a merge failure caused by incomplete data.

The data part is too old: if a data part is already very old, it may have been merged earlier by another replica and is no longer directly available, so ClickHouse may also download the merged result from another replica to reduce complexity and potential latency.

This metric shows the trade-off between keeping data consistent and optimizing resource usage (such as reducing network traffic and improving merge efficiency). Monitoring it helps to understand how the system balances these factors in practice, so that strategies or configuration can be adjusted for better performance and resource utilization.
在数据部分中，但不用于SELECT查询的部分\t\n: Parts that are in the data parts list but are not used for SELECT queries
延迟插入阻塞的平均等待时间: Average Wait Time of Delayed Inserts
当前数据库中经过压缩处理的数据分区的数量。这些分区通过算法减少了数据占用的空间，: Number of compact data parts in the current database. These parts reduce the space taken by the data while keeping the data complete and queryable.
所有表INSERT的字节数（未压缩列以它们在内存中存储的形式）: Bytes INSERTed into all tables (uncompressed columns in the form they are stored in memory)
所有重试后分布式连接失败总数: Total Distributed Connection Failures After All Retries
插入到MergeTree表的数据写入到文件系统的字节数: Bytes written to the file system for data inserted into MergeTree tables
插入到MergeTree表的未压缩字节数（列以它们在内存中存储的形式）: Uncompressed bytes inserted into MergeTree tables (columns in the form they are stored in memory)
数据部分在任何副本上都不存在的次数（即使是现在离线的副本）。这些数据部分肯定丢失: Number of times a data part does not exist on any replica (even on replicas that are currently offline). Such data parts are definitely lost. With asynchronous replication (if quorum inserts are not enabled) this is normal when the replica that wrote the data part fails and does not contain that part after coming back online
暂时保留、等待相关查询结束即可清理的数据分区的数量。: Number of data parts that are temporarily retained and can be cleaned up as soon as the related queries finish.
由于分区的活动数据部分数量过多，INSERT到MergeTree表的块被拒绝的次: Number of times INSERT blocks into MergeTree tables were rejected because there were too many active data parts in a partition
由于分区的活动数据部分数量过多，INSERT到MergeTree表的块被限制时的: Total wait time (in milliseconds) when INSERT blocks into MergeTree tables were throttled because there were too many active data parts in a partition
由于分区的活动数据部分数量过多，INSERT到MergeTree表的块被限制的次: Number of times INSERT blocks into MergeTree tables were throttled because there were too many active data parts in a partition
由于慢查询读取，降低查询处理线程数的次数\n\npeek\n先计算每2分钟的瞬时:
Number of times the number of query processing threads was reduced because of slow reads

peek
First computes the instantaneous rate every 2 minutes, then over a total time span of 1 hour (1h), using a 1 minute (1m) time slice, finds the maximum of all instantaneous rates computed within each slice. It therefore returns the maximum instantaneous read back-off event rate for every 1 minute slice over the last 1 hour.
目前正在生成的部分，不在数据部分列表中: Parts that are currently being generated and are not yet in the list of data parts
等待异步插入到 Distributed 表中的文件数量\n这个指标对于监控数据导:
Number of files waiting to be inserted asynchronously into Distributed tables
This metric is critical for monitoring the efficiency of data ingestion or asynchronous processing. A large value may mean that a lot of data is queued to be written into Distributed tables, which can affect data freshness or processing speed. A high value may also indicate a backlog of ingestion jobs, network bottlenecks, insufficient processing capacity of remote nodes or other performance issues related to the distributed system.

Monitoring ClickHouseMetrics_DistributedFilesToInsert helps to identify and diagnose bottlenecks in the data flow, so that optimizations can be applied, such as tuning the concurrency of insert jobs, optimizing the network configuration, increasing the processing capacity of the target tables or adjusting the configuration parameters of Distributed tables, to make sure that data is stored and processed by the distributed system efficiently and in time.
衡量的是向远程服务器发送数据的连接数量，这些数据最初是插入到 Distribut: Measures the number of connections that send data to remote servers, where that data was originally inserted into a Distributed table. A Distributed table is a special table type in ClickHouse used for distributed storage and processing; it does not store data itself but forwards data operations (such as INSERT queries) to the remote nodes defined by the table. Monitoring this metric shows how active data distribution is, that is, how frequently data flows inside the ClickHouse cluster, as well as the load of distributed queries or data loading operations. A high value may mean that a large amount of data is being processed or stored in a distributed way, which can be a sign of a busy system or of an efficient data pipeline. However, if this metric is abnormally high and is accompanied by performance problems, the network conditions, the processing capacity of the remote servers or the configuration of the Distributed tables may need further investigation in order to optimize data transfer efficiency and overall system performance.
记录了当前有多少个数据分区正处于这样一种待删除的状态——即这些分区已经被逻辑上标: Records how many data parts are currently in a pending-delete state, that is, parts that have been logically marked as 'pending cleanup' and whose physical deletion will be performed automatically by the system once the resources are released.
需要解释和可能执行的查询数量，不包括失败的查询: Number of queries to be interpreted and potentially executed, excluding failed queries
